response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r3c1 remains in critical survival crisis with SoC at 8.77 kWh (23.7% of 37 kWh capacity), islanded, and sustaining a 1.0 kW load that will deplete reserves in ~8.8 hours. The memory log reveals a synchronized cascade of urgent requests from three peers (r0c5, r3c2, r4c1) all arriving within the same time window, coupled with explicit rejections from r0c5 and r2c1 citing r3c1's sub-threshold SoC and cluster-wide scarcity. r2c1 has explicitly flagged r3c1 as distrusted, further isolating this household. The pattern suggests either a coordinated stress event across the cluster or a cascading failure mode. With no grid reconnection signal, no external supply arriving, and all nearby peers either unable or unwilling to provide relief, r3c1 must maintain absolute conservation posture. The survival margin (~3.5 kWh minimum) is now critically thin; any further SoC decline below 20% risks load shedding or complete blackout. Outbound sharing remains impossible and counterproductive—every kWh must be defended for household survival. Recovery depends entirely on grid restoration, load reduction, or external intervention.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.60
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers:
  - r2c1
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Critical survival mode: SoC 23.7% (8.77/37 kWh), islanded, 1.0 kW load, ~8.8 hours to depletion. All peers rejecting or requesting. r2c1 has distrusted r3c1. Zero outbound sharing until SoC recovers above 60%. Cluster-wide scarcity detected. Awaiting grid reconnection or external supply."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
